title: Supporting the designation of April 2025 as "National Native Plant Month".
plain_summary: This bill wants to make April 2025 National Native Plant Month. Native plants are plants that naturally grow in America without people bringing them from other places. These plants are very important because they help keep the environment healthy and provide food and homes for animals. Many native plants have disappeared because their homes have been destroyed and other plants from different countries have taken over their space. The bill wants people to learn about and celebrate these important American plants.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Resolution Supporting the designation of April 2025 as National Native Plant Month
sections.text: This resolution supports making April 2025 National Native Plant Month. Native plants are species that grew naturally in a place before people brought in plants from other areas. These plants evolved over time alongside the animals that live in the same area. The United States has more than seventeen thousand different types of native plants. These include big trees that give shade, smaller trees that grow under the big ones, bushes, plants that come back each year, climbing vines, grasses, and wildflowers. Native plants are very important for keeping our environment healthy and balanced. They help clean the air we breathe, filter the water we drink, and keep soil from washing away. These plants are perfectly suited to the specific soil, temperature, rainfall, and other conditions where they naturally grow. This makes them the best choice for protecting our environment and helping it adapt to changes. Native plants provide homes for wildlife and food like nectar, pollen, seeds, and leaves that native butterflies, caterpillars, birds, bees, and other animals need to survive. Plants brought from other places cannot provide the same benefits. However, more than two hundred types of native plants in the United States have disappeared since the early eighteen hundreds. The main reasons native plants are declining include losing their natural homes when land is developed, damage to their habitats, extreme weather, and invasive species that take over their space. Native plants are essential parts of strong ecosystems that can bounce back from problems, and they are an important part of our natural heritage. Therefore, the House of Representatives supports designating National Native Plant Month and recognizes how native plants benefit both the environment and the economy of the United States.
